title: White diagram background and remove labels from placed icons

requirements.summary: Change the attack path diagram canvas background color to white
acceptanceCriteria: The canvas/diagram area in AttackPathCanvas.tsx renders with a white (#ffffff) background.
The white background is visible in both normal and any overlay states.

file_operations.description: Update the canvas container element's styling to set the background color to white (#ffffff). Ensure the background is applied to the main interactive area where icons, drawings, and connections are rendered. Verify that the white background remains visible during all interaction states (drag, draw, select, erase, etc.).

requirements.summary: Remove text labels from icons after they are placed on the attack path diagram canvas
acceptanceCriteria: Icons placed on the diagram canvas (AttackPathCanvas.tsx) render without any text label.
The icon image is still rendered correctly at its correct position and size.
Icons in the toolbar (AttackPathToolbar.tsx) are not affected — they may still show labels if they currently do.
Existing diagrams loaded from the backend also display icons without labels.

file_operations.description: Modify the icon rendering logic within the canvas to suppress the display of text labels when icons are rendered on the diagram. Ensure that only the icon image is displayed at the correct position and size. The icon's name or label should not be rendered beneath or beside the icon image on the canvas. Verify that this change applies to both newly placed icons and icons loaded from existing saved diagrams. Do not modify the AttackPathIcon component itself; instead, control label visibility through props or conditional rendering within the canvas component.
